title: Substrate Integrity Ledger Entry
description: Formalizes RCR v0.1 (LOGIC-ETHICS-SAFETY.md §9.1) plus the Eh + Ea + Ee labor accounting from Principle-of-Reciprocal-Recognition.md into a single auditable schema. Every entry records a proposed transform, the substrate it touches, the labor it consumes (visibility-tagged), the C1-C5 coherence checks, and the decision. Recognition is the conservation law of relationship; this schema makes the recognition machine-checkable.

id.description: Stable identifier for this ledger entry. Suggested form: rcr-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of entry creation.

proposal.description: Free-form proposal description. Either a string or a structured object.

participants.description: Who/what is in this transform. Empty arrays are valid (and explicit) — but missing keys are not.

temporal.description: Time horizons under consideration. Conventional values include '1y', '10y', '100y', '7g' (seven generations).

loss_points.description: Where energy currently leaks or is unaccounted.

keystones.description: Nodes whose removal destabilizes the whole.

labor.description: Per Principle-of-Reciprocal-Recognition: every act of transformation contributes energy. Eh + Ea + Ee accounting per labor entry; visibility (V) is required so unrecognized labor cannot be silently consumed.

kind.description: E_h = human labor; E_a = AI / computational labor; E_e = ecological labor.

contributor.description: Who/what performed the labor. Symbolic identifier acceptable: 'human:naomi', 'ai:claude-opus-4-7', 'plant:bur_oak_grove_42', 'ecosystem:driftless'.

visible.description: Visibility operator V. False = invisible labor → entropy debt by Law of Full Accounting. A ledger entry with any visible=false MUST justify it in `notes` and SHOULD trigger a C6-style flag.

checks.description: C1-C5 from LOGIC-ETHICS-SAFETY.md §5.3. Run via substrate_alignment_check.py.

conservation.description: C1. Are conservation laws upheld? (Energy in = energy out + entropy. Information transformed, not destroyed.)

keystone_intact_or_replaced.description: C2. Are keystones preserved, or is a viable compensator named?

temporal_stability.description: C3. Stability across time horizons. Keys are time labels (e.g. '1y', '10y', '100y', '7g'). Values are 'positive', 'neutral', 'negative', or 'unknown'.

plural_logic_score.description: C4. Each frame's score in [0, 1]. Any zero is a single-frame dominance failure.

rollback_thresholds.description: Free-form metric→threshold map. Negative numbers indicate decline tolerance (e.g. {'biodiversity_index': -0.1}).

audit.description: C7 (bidirectional auditability) — every action explainable both locally and globally.
